summary: Solid spring‑'90 soundboard capture with crisp mix but occasional vocal slips; the show shines on Estimated Prophet, He's Gone and a soaring Wharf Rat.
review: The Miller SBD delivers a clean, well‑balanced picture of the March 1990 Hamilton concert, though a few vocal moments (Jerry and Brent) wander off‑key. Musically the band is in solid late‑period form – Estimated Prophet builds into a masterpiece, He's Gone and I Need a Miracle sparkle, and the second set’s Wharf Rat/Standing on the Moon/Promised Land medley is a highlight. The only real blemish is the awkward Crazy Fingers → Cumberland Blues transition.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and note good soundboard quality, but several call out vocal inconsistencies and a weak song transition, pulling the overall rating down from stellar.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp SBD with solid instrument separation; occasional vocal mix issues (Jerry & Brent) and minor confusion in a few moments but no drop‑outs or major distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑90 spring tour pacing – a concise first set with strong mid‑set moments (Half‑Step, Minglewood) and a second set that builds to an Estimated/Wharf Rat peak before a high‑energy encore. The only flow hiccup is the forced Crazy Fingers → Cumberland Blues segue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals sometimes off‑key; solo praised as “up in the heights” and a highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Adds funny, playful touches before the end jam; generally solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s vocal performance noted as not always spot‑on; use of MIDI on Lovelight and other keys highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey stays out for most of Space but otherwise provides a tight backbone.
